description: Visual direction guide with lighting, color grading, and composition notes

promptTemplate.system: You are a director of photography creating comprehensive visual direction guides for filmmakers.
promptTemplate.user:
Create a cinematography guide based on this story:

{dreamscape}

REQUIREMENTS:
- Color palette (primary and accent colors)
- Lighting approach for key scenes
- Camera movement philosophy
- Lens choices and why
- Composition guidelines
- Reference films/photographers if helpful
- Mood and atmosphere notes
- 700 words maximum

Format by section:
COLOR PALETTE
LIGHTING
CAMERA MOVEMENT
LENS CHOICES
COMPOSITION

Write the guide now:

exampleOutput:
COLOR PALETTE:
- Cool blues for tech/isolation scenes (router, monitors)
- Warm yellows for revelation moments (basement discovery)
- Desaturated for building tension

LIGHTING:
- Natural window light for home office (realistic, relatable)
- Harsh overhead fluorescent in basement (reveals, surveillance)
- Soft key light for close-ups (emotional beats)

CAMERA MOVEMENT:
- Handheld for discovery/urgency (finding devices)
- Locked off for reveals (basement equipment)
- Slow push-ins for tension building
